choices.text: storage (permanent, expensive), memory (temp, cheap), calldata (read-only function input, cheapest). State vars default to storage. Use calldata for external function inputs you won't modify.
Pack same-slot tiny types: uint128 + uint128 in 1 slot vs. 2 separate uint256 slots. Saves ~17k gas on initial write. Order struct fields by size for max packing.

questions.explanation: Pack tiny types in struct for slot savings.
